constructors.name: constructor(manifest: ApplicationManifest)
constructors.description: Initializes a new instance of the Application class from manifest.

parameters.name: manifest
parameters.description: The manifest used to create the application.

properties.name: readonly manifest: ApplicationManifest
properties.description: Gets the status of the application.

properties.name: readonly status: string
properties.description: Gets the status of the application.
properties.remarks: The application status can be <mark>NONE</mark>, <mark>LOADING</mark>, <mark>LOADED</mark>, or <mark>FAILED</mark>.

properties.name: readonly shortcut: TaskbarShortcut
properties.description: Gets the Taskbar shortcut of the application.

properties.name: readonly portal?: Portal
properties.description: Gets the portal of the application.
properties.remarks: The value is <mark>null</mark> if the application hasn't been added to the portal.

properties.name: readonly elementRoot?: HTMLElement
properties.description: Gets the the root content element of the application.
properties.remarks: The value is <mark>null</mark> if the application hasn't finished loading its HTML content.

properties.name: readonly error?: Error
properties.description: Gets the error that occurred while loading the application.

methods.name: load(): Promise<void>
methods.description: Loads the application. Subsequent calls return the previous Promise.

methods.name: addPostInitTask(init: (this: Application) => Promise<any> | any)
methods.description: Adds a post-initialization task to the application that executes after it loads.
methods.remarks: The post-initialization task can return a Promise for long-running jobs. The portal overlay will display a loading page until the task finishes.
